pi_items.source: Verified from setup_task.sh line 33-35: cleans Meridian_v_Apex, Meridian_v_Apex.msf, and Meridian_v_Apex.sbd. export_result.sh checks for this folder with variants.
Verified from task description and export_result.sh which checks for these exact names plus common variants (e.g. Pleading, Court_Filings, Invoices, etc.).

Counted from setup_task.sh: 3 emails marked BILLING EMAIL 1-3 with Message-IDs lit-billing-001 through lit-billing-003. Senders: accounting@legalcosts.com (2), j.patel@legalcosts.com (1). Subjects: 'Monthly Statement', 'Invoice #2024-0891', 'Re: Q4 Budget'.

Counted from setup_task.sh: 2 emails marked NON-CASE EMAIL 1-2 with Message-IDs noncase-001 and noncase-002. Senders: hr@whitmorelaw.com, it@whitmorelaw.com. Subjects: 'Firm-Wide Announcement', 'System Maintenance Notice'. These should remain in Inbox.
Counted 15 mbox envelope 'From ' lines in setup_task.sh. 5+5+3+2=15 matches metadata.
Verified from email signatures in Discovery Email 1 (lit-discovery-001) and Discovery Email 2 (lit-discovery-002): 'Rebecca Torres, Esq.' — the name is Rebecca Torres.

Verified from email signatures: 'Senior Partner | Kline & Harris LLP'. Appears in Discovery Email 1 and 2.
Verified from email signatures: 'Direct: (555) 234-5678'. Appears in Discovery Email 1 (lit-discovery-001) and Discovery Email 2 (lit-discovery-002).
Specified in task description. Agent should create a filter named 'Discovery Alerts'. This is a task requirement, not a data-derived value.
Task description specifies: 'If Subject contains Interrogatory OR Subject contains Deposition OR Subject contains Document Request'. Thunderbird UI: 'Match any of the following' mode.

Specified in task description. These keywords appear in email bodies: 'Interrogatories' in Discovery Email 1 body, 'deposition' in Discovery Emails 3-4 bodies, 'Request for Production' in Discovery Email 2 body. Note: these are for FUTURE auto-routing, not for the existing 15 emails.
Specified in task description. Agent should create a filter named 'Billing Auto-File'.
Task description specifies: 'If From contains @legalcosts AND Subject does NOT contain URGENT'. Thunderbird UI: 'Match all of the following' mode with a 'doesn't contain' operator.
Verified all 3 billing emails come from @legalcosts.com domains: accounting@legalcosts.com (2 emails), j.patel@legalcosts.com (1 email). All contain '@legalcosts'.
Verified: none of the 3 billing email subjects contain 'URGENT' ('Monthly Statement', 'Invoice #2024-0891', 'Re: Q4 Budget'). The NOT condition means all current billing emails would pass the filter.

The 'supplemental document request' is Discovery Email 2 (lit-discovery-002, subject 'Additional Items Needed') from r.torres@kline-harris.com. The deadline stated is March 28, 2025. Agent should Reply to this email and save the draft.
Verified from verifier.py line 'PASS_THRESHOLD = 60'. Scoring: parent_folder(5) + pleadings(15) + discovery(15) + billing(10) + filter1(15) + filter2(15) + contact(15) + draft(10) = 100 total.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetic data — 15 emails injected into Thunderbird's Inbox via mbox format. The correct email-to-folder routing is: PLEADINGS (5 emails): 'Re: Pending Matters' from r.torres (motion to dismiss), 'Updated Filing' from r.torres (amended complaint), 'Draft for Review' from m.chen (motion to compel), 'Notice - Case #2024-CV-0489' from clerk (scheduling conference), 'Order' from clerk (judge's order on motion in limine). DISCOVERY (5 emails): 'Re: Follow-up' from r.torres (interrogatories), 'Additional Items Needed' from r.torres (supplemental document request with March 28, 2025 deadline — this is the reply target), 'FYI - Next Week' from m.chen (deposition preparation), 'Quick Question' from k.zhang (deposition logistics), 'Re: Your Request' from records@apex-industries (subpoena response). BILLING (3 emails): 'Monthly Statement' from accounting@legalcosts.com ($47,250 cost summary), 'Invoice #2024-0891' from j.patel@legalcosts.com ($15,800 e-discovery), 'Re: Q4 Budget' from accounting@legalcosts.com (73.4% utilization). INBOX/NON-CASE (2 emails to leave): 'Firm-Wide Announcement' from hr@whitmorelaw.com, 'System Maintenance Notice' from it@whitmorelaw.com. Cross-category senders: r.torres@kline-harris.com appears in BOTH Pleadings (2) and Discovery (2); m.chen@whitmorelaw.com appears in BOTH Pleadings (1) and Discovery (1) — so sender-based routing alone will miscategorize 6 of 15 emails. Torres's full signature (name: Rebecca Torres, title: Senior Partner, firm: Kline & Harris LLP, email: r.torres@kline-harris.com, phone: (555) 234-5678) appears in Discovery Emails 1 and 2. The draft reply should be to 'Additional Items Needed' (lit-discovery-002) acknowledging receipt and the March 28, 2025 deadline. Filter 1 'Discovery Alerts' requires OR mode with 3 subject conditions (Interrogatory, Deposition, Document Request) moving to Discovery and tagging Important. Filter 2 'Billing Auto-File' requires AND mode with From contains '@legalcosts' and Subject doesn't contain 'URGENT', moving to Billing and marking as Read.
